channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Professor Binu Luke Appointed Chief Director of NWU Desmond Tutu School of Medicine
item.description: North West University has announced the appointment of Professor Binu Luke as the new Chief Director of the Desmond Tutu School of Medicine, effective 1 April 2026. Prof Luke is a highly experienced S...

item.title: Pakistan's Growing Case for Electric Cooking Amid Gas Shortages
item.description: Pakistan is facing increasing pressure on its household fuel system due to international gas supply disruptions, particularly after the Israel-Iran conflict disturbed LNG shipments through the Strait ...

item.title: Understanding How Love and Long Marriage Evolve Over Time
item.description: The article by Udo Maryanne Okonjo takes readers through her reflections after thirty years of marriage, exploring how assumptions, love languages, and values evolve in long-term relationships. She be...

item.title: Governor Okpebholo Encourages Edo Youths to Join Army Recruitment
item.description: Governor Monday Okpebholo of Edo State don call on eligible youths in the state to take advantage of the ongoing Nigerian Army 91 Regular Recruits Intake. The recruitment process go run from April 8 t...

item.title: CFO to increase online orientation sessions for future OFWs amidst rising costs
item.description: The Commission on Filipinos Overseas (CFO) is set to expand its online Pre-Departure Orientation Seminar (PDOS) services starting Monday, April 13, 2026. With the ongoing fuel crisis affecting travel ...

item.title: High Court Directs IG to Act on Delayed Kilifi Estate Fraud Arrests
item.description: The High Court has put the Inspector General of Police on the spot, ordering him to explain why arrests have not been made in a long-running Kilifi estate fraud case. The matter involves a dispute ove...

item.title: Serengeti Girls arrive in Botswana ready for U-17 World Cup qualifying match
item.description: Tanzania’s national Under-17 women’s football team, popularly known as the Serengeti Girls, have safely arrived in Francistown, Botswana, where they will face the Botswana team this Saturday in the fi...

item.title: Supreme Court orders Heera Group MD Nowhera Shaik to surrender and cooperate in ₹10,000 crore fraud probe
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has directed Nowhera Shaik, managing director of Heera Gold Exim Private Limited, to surrender within one week in connection with a massive gold Ponzi scam that allegedly de...

item.title: Canadian Army Evaluates Armoured Vehicle Modernization Amid Drone Threats
item.description: The Canadian Army is accelerating plans to modernize its armoured vehicles in response to lessons learned from Ukraine’s drone-dominated battlefield. Aging Leopard 2A4 and 2A6M tanks require replaceme...

item.title: Heavy Rains Cause Deaths and Injuries in Multiple Districts of Punjab
item.description: Over the past 24 hours, Punjab has experienced heavy rainfall and storms resulting in multiple casualties and injuries. In Bhakkar, two roof-collapse incidents caused one death and four injuries. Khan...

item.title: AmaZulu coach Zwane disappointed as Usuthu share points with Sekhukhune United
item.description: AmaZulu coach Arthur Zwane expressed his frustration after his team, Usuthu, squandered a two-goal lead to draw 2-2 with Sekhukhune United on Monday. Usuthu initially went ahead through Thapelo Matlho...

item.title: Arthur Zwane disappointed as AmaZulu draw with Sekhukhune United
item.description: AmaZulu coach Arthur Zwane expressed his frustration after his team drew 2-2 with Sekhukhune United on Monday, despite leading twice during the match. Usuthu initially went ahead through Thapelo Matlh...

item.title: Mamata Banerjee challenges electoral roll freeze in Supreme Court during Bhabanipur nomination filing
item.description: On Wednesday, West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee filed her nomination from the Bhabanipur constituency and announced that her party, the Trinamool Congress (TMC), will move the Supreme Court a...

item.title: Halifax reports March decline in UK house prices as rising mortgage rates affect buyers' confidence
item.description: According to data released by Halifax, the average price of a UK home fell by 0.5% in March 2026, bringing the average property value to £299,677. This decline contrasts with a modest rise of 0.3% rec...

item.title: South-South APC Elects New Zonal Officers to Strengthen Unity Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) for South-South don elect new Zonal Executive Committee for the region, after one smooth congress wey hold for Asaba, Delta State. Top party leaders, including the ...

item.title: Squamish mayor condemns dangerous stunt involving Volkswagen suspended on mountain
item.description: Authorities and residents in Squamish, British Columbia, are expressing alarm after a Volkswagen vehicle was discovered hanging off a cliffside near Stawamus Chief Mountain. The car was suspended by c...

item.title: Iran Embassy Responds to Kenya Over Gulf Attacks
item.description: Kenya's Foreign Affairs PS Korir Sing’oei recently held talks with the UAE’s Assistant Minister of Foreign Affairs for Energy and Sustainability, Abdulla Balalaa, expressing concern over Iran’s milita...

item.title: Kerala 2026 Elections: Key Aspirations of Young and First-Time Voters
item.description: As Kerala prepares for its 2026 assembly elections, the focus has shifted towards understanding the aspirations of the state’s young and first-time voters. Education has emerged as one of the central ...

item.title: ACCI outlines livestock development plan to create 350,000 jobs in two years
item.description: The President of the Abuja Chamber of Commerce and Industry (ACCI), Dr. Akajiugo Emeka Obegolu SAN, don talk say the new livestock development plan fit generate near 350,000 jobs within di first two y...

item.title: MK Party and ATM call for greater judicial accountability through lifestyle audits
item.description: The MK Party and African Transformation Movement (ATM) are calling for increased scrutiny of judges, advocating for lifestyle audits similar to those conducted on members of parliament. The parties ar...
